summary: Full backend + frontend regression run for NetMon app. 23/23 backend pytest cases pass covering health, auth (login/register/logout/me), devices CRUD, metrics (overview/traffic/device), alerts (list/filter/ack/resolve), topology, logs, users, agents (create/heartbeat/install-script), RBAC (viewer 403). Frontend Playwright verified: login flow, dashboard KPIs with real values, traffic-chart on dashboard, recent alerts/activity, Devices table (13 rows) + add dialog, Traffic charts render, Alerts filters clickable, Topology SVG renders 13 nodes/12 links, Users role selects (5), Agents page (add/download/install script visible), Settings theme+lang toggles work (html class 'dark' removed on light). No console/page errors.

ui_bugs.issue: Missing data-testid="traffic-chart" on the main AreaChart container in /app/frontend/src/pages/Traffic.jsx. Chart renders correctly, but the review requirement expects the testid on /traffic. Only Dashboard has it.

action_items: Add data-testid="traffic-chart" to the ResponsiveContainer wrapping the AreaChart in Traffic.jsx (line ~77) so /traffic page matches the contract used by Dashboard.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 823 lines - consider splitting into modules (auth, devices, metrics, alerts, topology, agents) for maintainability.
Login response returns user directly (no wrapping in {user: ...}); frontend seems to handle this fine but document the shape.
Device schema uses hostname + ip_address (not name/ip) - keep contract consistent across API and clients.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed devices/agents/users; devices/agents cleaned up in test. TEST_ users remain in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend uses httpOnly cookies (access_token+refresh_token) - use requests.Session and cookies persist. Login/register/me all return the raw user object (not wrapped). Device fields: hostname, ip_address. Metrics overview key is current_bandwidth_mbps (not bandwidth). All 23 pytest tests pass in ~4s. Frontend all core pages verified with data-testids.
